poster_title: Expanding RL with Verifiable Rewards Across Diverse Domains
authors: Yi Su, Dian Yu, Linfeng Song, Juntao Li, Haitao Mi, Zhaopeng Tu, Min Zhang, Dong Yu

content: RLVR improves reasoning capabilities of LLMs in structured tasks.
Challenges in verifying complex, unstructured reference answers.
Extension to broader domains is unexplored.
Binary verification signals are limited in unstructured tasks.
Proposed framework addresses these limitations.
Complex, unstructured reference answers in diverse domains.
Limited binary verification signals for unstructured tasks.
Need for a more flexible and scalable reward mechanism.
Challenges in training verifiers without extensive domain-specific annotation.
Solution: Generative scoring technique with soft, model-based reward signals.
Generative scoring technique for soft, model-based reward signals.
Training cross-domain generative reward models using 7B LLMs.
Using noisy datasets under supervision of a larger teacher model.
panels.figure_caption: Overview paradigm of RLVR with our cross-domain verifier.

content: Comprehensive experiments across diverse domains: medicine, chemistry, psychology, economics, education.
Evaluation using free-form reasoning tasks.
Performance compared against state-of-the-art models.
Observed robust policies with model-based soft rewards.
Released dataset of 570k multi-domain examples.
Significant performance gains over Qwen2.5-72B and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B.
Model-based soft rewards scale better and produce more robust policies.
Feasibility of training compact cross-domain verifiers without extensive annotation.
Released dataset and trained reward model for future research.
Demonstrated practical RL applications in real-world domains.
Soft rewards enhance robustness and scalability of RL in noisy-label scenarios.
Cross-domain verifier can be trained with relatively small LLMs.
Generative scoring technique is effective in diverse domains.
Released dataset and model facilitate further research.
Practical RL applications in real-world domains are closer to reality.
Proposed RLVR framework with soft rewards significantly enhances robustness, flexibility, and scalability.
Substantial step towards practical RL applications in diverse, real-world domains.
Future work: Addressing challenges in extremely unstructured or ambiguous tasks.
Continued exploration of generative scoring techniques.
Expanding the framework to more domains and applications.
